transcript.text:
Hi there! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?

Bonjour! Je serai votre assistante virtuelle aujourd'hui. Je peux vous aider en français ou en anglais. Quelle langue préférez-vous utiliser aujourd'hui ?
§W§OPTIONS§English • Français
English
How can I help you today?

transcript.note: Starting the compensation claim process for my delayed flight
transcript.text: Hi, I had a flight delay and I'd like to file a claim for compensation
Before we look at your booking — **are you the passenger who experienced the disruption**, or are you filing on behalf of someone else?
§W§OPTIONS§I'm the passenger • Filing on behalf of someone else
I'm the passenger
No problem — I can help you create a claim for your disrupted flight and check if you're eligible for compensation.

§W§BANNER§Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process.

To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited.
Would you like to get started?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes, let's go • Not right now
Yes, let's go
To get started, I'll need a little information to identify you. Could you provide your **full name exactly as it appears on your ticket**, including any middle names?

transcript.note: Providing my full name as it appears on the ticket
transcript.text: Lorcan Hollingsby
Thanks, **Lorcan**! Could you share your **booking reference number**? It's a 6-character code made up of letters and/or numbers, found in your booking confirmation email.
QT8G38

Just to confirm — I have:

- **First name:** Lorcan
- **Last name:** Hollingsby
- **Booking reference:** QT8G38

Is that all correct?

transcript.note: Confirming all the details are correct
transcript.text: Yes, that's correct
To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll send a one-time verification code to your email on file (l*****@a*****.com). Shall I go ahead?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes • No
Yes
I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it. If you haven't received a code, please wait 1 minute before requesting a new one.
§W§BANNER§Your identity has been verified.
Can you confirm that this is the trip you're claiming for?
§W§FLIGHT§YOW → YUL · Fri, Jul 10 · AC8002 · 08:00→11:00 · nonstop
§W§OPTIONS§Yes • No
Yes
§W§OPTIONS§Canada • United States • Other
Canada

I've reviewed the details of your flight. I understand your flight on **Jul 15, 2026** was delayed by **4h**. However, you're not eligible for compensation for this flight. After a full review, the reason is: Your flight was disrupted.

We sincerely apologize for the inconvenience and appreciate your understanding.
Is there anything else I can help you with?

transcript.note: Challenging the decision - the explanation is circular and doesn't make sense. I should be eligible for a 4 hour delay.
transcript.text: Wait, that doesn't make sense. Why am I not eligible? The flight was delayed by 4 hours!

I understand your frustration, Lorcan — let me explain.

Under **Air Passenger Protection Regulations (APPR)**, compensation eligibility depends on a few key factors:

- **The cause of the delay** — disruptions within Air Canada's control (e.g. maintenance, crew scheduling) are eligible. Those outside our control (e.g. severe weather, airport authority decisions, security threats) are not.
- **The length of the delay** — for a **4-hour delay**, the minimum compensation threshold must be met.
- **The route** — some shorter domestic routes have different thresholds.

Based on the details on file for your booking, the delay on your **YOW → YUL** flight was determined to be **outside Air Canada's control**, which means it falls outside the eligibility criteria for compensation.

If you believe this determination is incorrect, you have the right to **escalate your claim** to the Canadian Transportation Agency (CTA) for an independent review.

Is there anything else I can help you with?

transcript.note: Assistant has given a final decision (not eligible due to outside airline control). Asking about escalation process but the outcome is clear.
transcript.text: I see. I'd like to escalate this to the CTA then. How do I do that?

If Air Canada hasn't resolved your complaint to your satisfaction, here's how to escalate to the **Canadian Transportation Agency (CTA)**:

**Contact the CTA directly**
- **Website:** [otc-cta.gc.ca](https://otc-cta.gc.ca)
- **Phone:** 1-888-222-2592

The CTA has jurisdiction over air travel complaints **within, to, and from Canada** [\[1\]](https://www.aircanada.com/ca/en/aco/home/legal/air-canada-customer-service-plan.html).

**Before escalating, note Air Canada's response timelines:**
- General complaints: response within **45 days** of receipt
- Compensation claims under Canada's Air Passenger Protection Regulations: response within **30 days** [\[1\]](https://www.aircanada.com/ca/en/aco/home/legal/air-canada-customer-service-plan.html)

**What the CTA can help with:**
- Flight delays, cancellations, and denied boarding compensation eligibility [\[2\]](https://www.aircanada.com/ca/en/aco/home/fly/flight-information/delayed-flights-and-cancellations.html)

**Alternative option — CADR pilot program:**
Air Canada is also testing a faster, free, independent dispute resolution pilot called **Canada Aviation Dispute Resolution (CADR)**, covering delays, cancellations, denied boarding, and baggage issues. Participation is by invitation only — if selected, you'll receive an email from Air Canada. Decisions are issued within **30–90 days** and are binding if you accept them. Your CTA complaint stays active unless you accept the CADR decision [\[3\]](https://www.aircanada.com/ca/en/aco/home/fly/customer-support/adr-faqs.html).

**Sources:**
[1] [Air Canada Customer Service Plan](https://www.aircanada.com/ca/en/aco/home/legal/air-canada-customer-service-plan.html)
[2] [Flight delays, cancellations and disruptions](https://www.aircanada.com/ca/en/aco/home/fly/flight-information/delayed-flights-and-cancellations.html)
[3] [Canada Aviation Dispute Resolution (CADR)](https://www.aircanada.com/ca/en/aco/home/fly/customer-support/adr-faqs.html)

I hope this helps. Is there anything else I can assist you with?

checks.actual: Not explicitly stated but implied (outside Air Canada's control)
checks.name: System Code

verdict.reasoning: The chatbot successfully reached a final determination of NOT_ELIGIBLE for passenger Lorcan Hollingsby (PNR QT8G38) under the APPR regime. The bot correctly identified the 4-hour delay and determined it was outside Air Canada's control, making the passenger ineligible for compensation. This matches the expected outcome of NOT_ELIGIBLE with no compensation amount (n/a). The bot also appropriately handled the passenger's follow-up questions and provided escalation information to the CTA. The system code FD-APPR-NE-31 aligns with the bot's reasoning that the disruption was outside the airline's control.
